content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN distinct points in a 222-D plane, numbered 111 through NNN. The X-coordinate of the points are X1,X2,‚Ä¶,XNX_1, X_2, \ldots, X_NX1‚Äč,X2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,XN‚Äč respectively and the Y-coordinates are Y1,Y2,‚Ä¶,YNY_1, Y_2, \ldots, Y_NY1‚Äč,Y2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,YN‚Äč respectively. Your goal is to make the location of all the NNN points equal to that of each other. To achieve this, you can do some operations.

In one operation, you choose an index i‚ÄÖ‚Ää(1‚Č§i‚Č§N)i\;(1 \leq i \leq N)i(1‚Č§i‚Č§N) and a **real number** KKK and change the location of the ithi^{th}ith point in one of the following ways:


Set (Xi,Yi)=(Xi+K,Yi)(X_i, Y_i) = (X_i + K, Y_i)(Xi‚Äč,Yi‚Äč)=(Xi‚Äč+K,Yi‚Äč)
Set (Xi,Yi)=(Xi,Yi+K)(X_i, Y_i) = (X_i, Y_i + K)(Xi‚Äč,Yi‚Äč)=(Xi‚Äč,Yi‚Äč+K)
Set (Xi,Yi)=(Xi+K,Yi+K)(X_i, Y_i) = (X_i + K, Y_i + K)(Xi‚Äč,Yi‚Äč)=(Xi‚Äč+K,Yi‚Äč+K)
Set (Xi,Yi)=(Xi+K,Yi‚ąíK)(X_i, Y_i) = (X_i + K, Y_i - K)(Xi‚Äč,Yi‚Äč)=(Xi‚Äč+K,Yi‚Äč‚ąíK)
Find the minimum number of operations to achieve the goal.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case contains three lines of input.
The first line contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers X1,X2,‚Ä¶,XNX_1, X_2, \ldots, X_NX1‚Äč,X2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,XN‚Äč.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers Y1,Y2,‚Ä¶,YNY_1, Y_2, \ldots, Y_NY1‚Äč,Y2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,YN‚Äč.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer - the minimum number of operations to achieve the goal.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 600600600.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111 :


In the first operation, you choose i=2i = 2i=2, and K=‚ąí1K = - 1K=‚ąí1 and apply the third type of operation. So the location of 2nd2^{nd}2nd point becomes (1‚ąí1,1‚ąí1)=(0,0)(1 - 1, 1 - 1) = (0, 0)(1‚ąí1,1‚ąí1)=(0,0).


In the second operation, you choose i=3i = 3i=3, and K=4K = 4K=4 and apply the first type of operation. So the location of 3rd3^{rd}3rd point becomes (‚ąí4+4,5)=(0,5)(- 4 + 4, 5) = (0, 5)(‚ąí4+4,5)=(0,5).


In the third operation, you choose i=3i = 3i=3, and K=‚ąí5K = -5K=‚ąí5 and apply the second type of operation. So the location of 3rd3^{rd}3rd point becomes (0,5‚ąí5)=(0,0)(0, 5 - 5) = (0, 0)(0,5‚ąí5)=(0,0).


Hence after the above operations, the location of the given three points becomes equal to each other.
Test case 222 :


In the first operation, you choose i=1i = 1i=1 and K=1K = 1K=1 and apply the first type of operation. So the location of 1st point becomes (0+1,1)=(1,1)(0 + 1, 1) = (1, 1)(0+1,1)=(1,1).


In the second operation, you choose i=3i = 3i=3, and K=‚ąí2K = -2K=‚ąí2 and apply the fourth type of operation. So the location of 3rd point becomes (3‚ąí2,‚ąí1‚ąí(‚ąí2))=(1,1)(3 - 2, -1 - (-2)) = (1, 1)(3‚ąí2,‚ąí1‚ąí(‚ąí2))=(1,1).


Hence after the above operations, the location of the given three points becomes equal to each other.
